persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: this is a single-initial 'J. Donnan', so a same-name relative or a successor in the harbour post could hold the stint, and the deterministic edition-cooccurrence corroboration was NOT met. Why it fails: there is only one Donnan official in the entire corpus's staff lists, and 'Master Attendant' of Colombo harbour is a unique senior office held by one man at a time; the age (40 in 1877) fits a man born 1837 who was master attendant from 1863, and the stint's last listing (1900) precedes the bio's retirement (1902). The residual single-initial risk plus the missing edition corroboration keep this at probable rather than certain.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second Donnan holding the Colombo master-attendant post, or a birth/death date inconsistent with master-attendant service 1863-1902.

notes: Single Donnan official in the corpus; unique senior post (Master Attendant, Colombo) and continuous career shape carry the link despite the single-initial flag and absent edition cooccurrence.
